$note: Eli Protocol playbook (eli_protocol.md). JSON to keep the loader stdlib-only. Every playbook carries all four phases (prevent/detect/respond/recover).

detect.signal: static scan for verify=False / disabled cert validation on TLS clients
detect.action: open ticket, flag the calling code path

respond: re-enable certificate verification on the flagged client and pin the trusted CA bundle
treat any data exchanged over the unverified channel as exposed to a MITM until proven otherwise
if the endpoint used a self-signed cert as the reason, add that cert to the trust store instead of disabling verification

harden.change: remove verify=False; use the system/CA bundle (or an explicit pinned CA) for TLS
add a lint/CI rule that fails on verify=False and equivalent cert-validation bypasses

recover: confirm the client now rejects an invalid/expired certificate (negative test)
rotate any credential or token that transited the unverified channel
